title: Senior engineering is force multiplication
hook: At some point the job stops being only about how much code you personally ship and starts being about how much better the system ships.

beats.heading: More tickets is not the whole ladder

blocks.html: A senior engineer still writes code. But the visible difference is judgment: picking the right problem, reducing risk, raising the quality bar, and making other engineers faster.

blocks.title: impact · wider than commits
blocks.caption: The useful signal is usually already there.

lines.t: unblocked mobile team with API contract

blocks.html: Find the bottleneck the team keeps tripping over: unclear ownership, slow reviews, brittle deploys, missing docs, or risky architecture. Then make that thing better.

blocks.code:
- Own ambiguous problems
- Create reusable patterns
- Teach through docs and reviews
- Remove repeated sources of failure
blocks.caption: Scope is often measured by repeated problems removed.

blocks.html: Track outcomes, not just effort. The work that multiplies others is easy to undercount unless you write down what changed.

items.label: forms of impact: code, judgment, leverage

blocks.html: The career move is not to become important everywhere. It is to become reliable where the team most needs judgment.

items.title: What do staff engineers actually do?

sources.title: What do staff engineers actually do?
